codeReview.summary: This was a verification-only feature. The previous feature (memory-db-extraction, commit 507d4f3) already replaced all bare sqlite3.connect() calls with get_conn() imports. The worker correctly verified this by running grep checks, import checks, and pragma verification. The commit d3a5675 is an empty commit (--allow-empty) since no code changes were needed — all verification steps passed. Current codebase state confirmed: (1) sqlite3.connect() appears only in src/memory_db.py:56 inside get_conn(), (2) all 8 consumer modules (curate.py, inject.py, entities.py, ingest.py, distill.py, embed.py, claude_recall.py, mcp_server.py) import and use get_conn() from src.memory_db, (3) bin/claude-recall is a thin wrapper with no direct DB access, (4) remaining sqlite3 imports in consumer modules are correctly used only for exception types (OperationalError, IntegrityError), (5) get_conn() properly sets WAL mode, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000, and row_factory=sqlite3.Row.

issues.description: The commit is empty (--allow-empty). This is technically correct for a verification-only feature where no code changes were needed, but it creates a no-op commit in history. A minor style concern — some projects prefer not to have empty commits. However, the feature description required a specific commit message, and the worker followed instructions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: For verification-only features where the previous worker already completed the implementation, the feature description could explicitly note this is a verification pass rather than implementation work. The worker's skillFeedback also noted this. This would prevent confusion about empty commits and set clearer expectations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description says 'Fix any remaining bare sqlite3.connect() calls' implying implementation work, but the precondition notes 'Previous feature attempted to replace all bare connects'. Worker created an --allow-empty commit since nothing needed fixing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The consumer modules use a dual-import pattern (try: from src.memory_db import get_conn / except: from memory_db import get_conn) to support both running from repo root and running from src/ directory. This pattern is present in all 8 consumer files but is not documented in AGENTS.md or architecture.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: curate.py:17-19, inject.py:25-27, entities.py:13-15, ingest.py:14-16, distill.py:18-20, embed.py:11-13 all use the try/except dual import pattern.

summary: PASS. The feature was a verification-only pass confirming that the prior memory-db-extraction feature successfully eliminated all bare sqlite3.connect() calls outside memory_db.py. All 8 consumer modules correctly import get_conn() from src.memory_db. The get_conn() function properly sets WAL mode, busy_timeout=5000, synchronous=NORMAL, and row_factory. The worker's verification was thorough — running grep checks, import verification, and pragma tests on both in-memory and file-based databases. The only artifact is an empty commit, which is acceptable given no code changes were required.
